pages.raw_text:
Tonight at Masonic temple the
telegraphers' union will forget such
trifling incidents as strikes in music
and dancing and a crowd that will
tax the capacity of the floor will
help them make merry.


Everybody has given the oper-
ators the best OF encouragement
and the sale OF tickets for the strike
benefit ball would tickle the box
Office of the biggest entertainment
enterprise going. The call for tick
cts has come in from all over the
Inland Empire and the sale in this
city went beyond the operators
most sanguine expectations. They'll
be feeling very well, thank you,
after the proceeds are counted up.
Nearly everybody has bought tick
cts except the strike breakers
they are finding their amusements
down at the almost deserted offlces.


The ball tonight will be in charge
of the following members Of the
union:


Floor manager, A. Nisonger; floor
director, H. c. Ingram; floor COM
mittee, A. Nisonger. A. w. Neimyer,
G. w. Brady. August Miller, c. l..
Clark, Joseph Bench; reception, j.
J. Fuller; refreshment, Mrs. A..
Thiede, Miss Lulu Reid, Mrs. A. w.
Neimyer.
